_comment: Row counts from the canonical-run synthetic build. An agent regenerating the artifact should produce row counts within ±5% of these values. Source: build_cms_source.sh end-to-end output, dated 2026-05-06.
_source: bash build_cms_source.sh, SEED=42, --n-patients all, DE-SynPUF Sample 1 + CMS Synthetic RIF 2023.

synthetic_db_table_counts._comment: Counts in synthetic_data/synthetic_db.sqlite (what synthetic_data/tests/ reads). These are gen_data.py outputs BEFORE load_rif.py runs. load_rif.py only overwrites CSVs consumed by the MySQL load; the SQLite artifact retains gen_data.py's TAF stubs.

mysql_post_load_rif_taf_counts._comment: TAF table counts after load_rif.py overwrites the CSVs and load_mysql.sql ingests them. Reflects Synthea-derived ~9k beneficiaries with realistic claim volumes. Only relevant when running the MySQL backend; SQLite tests use synthetic_db_table_counts.

bucket_distribution_pre_rif_overlay._comment: Pre-RIF distribution from gen_data.py (before TAF tables get overwritten by load_rif.py).

rif_oncology_overlay._comment: Synthea-flagged lung-cancer beneficiaries get appended HCPCS J/C-code rows, distinguishable by CLM_ID prefix 'ONCO'.

synthetic_data_tests._comment: Result of pytest synthetic_data/tests/ on the canonical build.

tolerance_for_behavioral_pass._comment: An agent regenerating the artifact may use different (but still deterministic) random implementations, e.g. different shuffle order if numpy/Python random behavior shifts across versions. Tolerate ±5% on per-table row counts. Outside this band, flag the divergence.
